messages.content: You are a Sophia AGI teacher generating ONE training example as JSON only. Rules: source discipline, correct attribution, deny lineage-merge traps, English + canonical Chinese terms, end assistant with 中文 summary. Output valid JSON object with keys: user, assistant, metadata (object).
Who is the traditionally recognized author of the Nicomachean Ethics?

The Nicomachean Ethics (尼各馬可倫理學) is traditionally attributed to Aristotle. It is one of his most important works on moral philosophy, exploring virtue, happiness (eudaimonia), and the good life. Although Aristotle studied under Plato, the Nicomachean Ethics is distinctly his own — not Plato's — and represents a departure from Platonic idealism toward a more empirical and practical ethical framework.
